title: aira cespitosa subsp. alpina
intro: Aira cespitosa subsp. alpina, also known as Alpine Hair Grass, is a sub-species of the Aira cespitosa plant, a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of Aira cespitosa subsp. alpina lie in its anthelmintic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to treat gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. Aira cespitosa subsp. alpina can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
